framework.name: RWA HQLA Internal Limits Matrix
framework.disclaimer: These are starting-point analytical proposals, not regulatory limits. Each institution's ALM committee should re-calibrate based on size, complexity, risk appetite, existing liquid asset diversification, and supervisor dialogue.

single_product.rationale: Avoid material exposure to a single wrapper plus issuer combination (e.g. BUIDL alone).
single_product.example: For a bank with EUR 100bn liquid assets, max EUR 5bn in any single tokenised product.

single_issuer.rationale: Avoid management concentration across products from the same issuer (e.g. all BlackRock-managed tokenised products combined).
single_issuer.example: For a bank with EUR 100bn liquid assets, max EUR 10bn across all products from one issuer.

single_chain.rationale: Diversify chain failure risk. BUIDL is ~95% on Ethereum, creating single-chain dependency.
single_chain.example: Within the tokenised RWA bucket, max 25% deployed on any single blockchain.

single_custodian.rationale: Diversify operational custody risk across institutional custodians (Anchorage, Fireblocks, Coinbase Custody).
single_custodian.example: Within the tokenised RWA bucket, max 33% under any single custody provider.

aggregate.rationale: Until products reach L1 HQLA status, tokenised treasuries should be a yield-enhancement allocation, not a structural liquidity reserve.
aggregate.example: For a bank with EUR 100bn liquid assets, max EUR 1-2bn aggregate tokenised RWA exposure.

calibration_guidance.gsib: Tighten further, e.g. 0.5% aggregate cap given systemic relevance.
calibration_guidance.fintech_neobank: Not bound by LCR; use as inspiration, adapt to cash management context.

exit_triggers.action: immediate liquidation review + emergency haircut overlay +15-20pp

exit_triggers.threshold: NAV vs on-chain price divergence > 1% sustained
any confirmed key compromise or unauthorized access
issuer invokes any extraordinary event or liability cap clause (e.g. bIB01 Article XVII)
aggregate exposure > 2.5% of liquid assets (10% over policy cap)
exit_triggers.action: rebalance to policy cap within 5 business days
immediate liquidation review for affected products

L1_ucits_mmf_achieved.rationale: UCITS MMF restructuration materially reduces wrapper risk

L2_ecb_collateral_eligible.rationale: ECB collateral eligibility (operational since 30 March 2026) reduces settlement finality risk
